  "riskReason": "Critical HTTP origin validation bypass in embed.js and potential unvalidated URL handling in topic retrieval.",
  "circularTests": {

  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "critical",
      "category": "Security - Origin Validation Bypass",
      "file": "app/assets/javascripts/embed.js",
      "line": 17,
      "title": "Insufficient postMessage origin validation",
      "description": "The postMessage origin check uses `indexOf()` which is insufficient for security validation. The check `discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) === -1` will fail to properly validate origins. For example, if discourseUrl is 'https://example.com', an attacker can send messages from 'attacker-example.com' and pass validation because 'example.com' is a substring match. This allows arbitrary cross-origin frame communication and potential XSS attacks.",
      "suggestion": "Use strict origin validation: `if (e.origin !== discourseUrl.replace(/\\/$/, '')) { return; }` or similar URL comparison that accounts for protocol and domain boundaries."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Security - Unvalidated URL in External Data Fetch",
      "file": "app/jobs/scheduled/poll_feed.rb",
      "line": 29,
      "title": "Unvalidated URL passed to open() for remote fetch",
      "description": "The code uses `open(SiteSetting.feed_polling_url)` without URL validation. An attacker who can modify SiteSetting.feed_polling_url could cause SSRF attacks (internal network scanning, metadata service access on cloud platforms). Additionally, the regex validation `url !~ /^https?\\:\\/\\/` uses a weak pattern that doesn't prevent malformed URLs.",
      "suggestion": "Validate feed_polling_url before use: parse with URI module, verify protocol is http/https, check against whitelist of allowed domains, and use a proper HTTP client (Net::HTTP or similar) instead of open-uri with timeouts."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Security - Unvalidated HTML Content Injection",
      "file": "app/jobs/scheduled/poll_feed.rb",
      "line": 35,
      "title": "Raw HTML from external feed stored without sanitization",
      "description": "Content from RSS feed items (`i.content`) is only unescaped with CGI.unescapeHTML and .scrub, then passed to TopicEmbed.import(). This content is likely stored and rendered as raw HTML (given the new `raw_html` cook method in Post.rb), creating XSS vulnerabilities. External feed content should be sanitized before storage.",
      "suggestion": "Sanitize feed content using a proper HTML sanitizer (e.g., Rails sanitize helper or Loofah) before passing to TopicEmbed.import(). Only allow safe HTML tags and attributes."
    }
  ]
